# Requirements Checklist: Portfolio MVP

**Purpose**: Validate whether the `portfolio-mvp` MVP1 requirements are complete, clear, consistent, measurable, and aligned with the ratified constitution without reopening locked decisions unless a real contradiction exists.
**Created**: 2026-06-08
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` workflow based on feature context and repository governance.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are the homepage requirements complete about which content must remain above the fold versus merely present on the page, especially for positioning, proof metrics, and the two primary CTAs? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 1, §FR-003, §FR-005, §SC-003]
- [ ] CHK002 Are the selected-project-card requirements complete enough to define the required proof statement and status content for all three cards, not just their titles and navigation state? [Completeness, Spec §FR-011, §FR-012, §FR-033]
- [ ] CHK003 Are route-level SEO and metadata requirements defined for every MVP1 public route, rather than only at release level? [Gap, Spec §FR-028, §FR-002]
- [ ] CHK004 Are contact requirements complete enough to specify display labels, ordering, and locale treatment for email, LinkedIn, GitHub, and Telegram across homepage and resume surfaces? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009, §FR-020, §Key Entities]
- [ ] CHK005 Are the `Portfolio CMS` case-page requirements complete about what public-safe evidence may appear inside `Architecture decisions` and `What it demonstrates` without exposing internal or unverifiable details? [Completeness, Spec §FR-013, §Scope & Trust Guardrails]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK006 Is “two equal primary actions” quantified with specific visual or hierarchy criteria so reviewers can assess equality objectively? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005]
- [ ] CHK007 Is “short” resume scope defined with objective limits beyond the one-page print target, such as allowable density or section depth? [Clarity, Spec §FR-019, §FR-021]
- [ ] CHK008 Is “basic SEO and metadata” clarified with a minimum required field set so implementation does not guess what counts as sufficient? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-028, §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK009 Is “honest and implementation-focused” role framing for commercial proof specific enough to prevent inflated wording across EN and UK versions? [Clarity, Spec §FR-018, §FR-024]
- [ ] CHK010 Is EN/UK parity defined precisely enough to distinguish what may vary in phrasing from what must remain structurally identical? [Clarity, Spec §FR-024, §SC-008, §Scope & Trust Guardrails]
- [ ] CHK011 Is the Clean Editorial Technical contract translated into specific MVP1 requirement language for restrained motion, hierarchy, and sharp geometry, rather than referenced only at a high level? [Gap, Spec §FR-029, DESIGN.md §Non-Goals, §Visual Direction, §Component Rules]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK012 Do the route requirements align across the scope guardrails, functional requirements, and success criteria with no missing or extra MVP1 route? [Consistency, Spec §Scope & Trust Guardrails, §FR-002, §SC-001, §SC-002]
- [ ] CHK013 Are the `coming next` constraints consistent across User Story 3, FR-014, FR-033, and SC-004 so `LMS` and `Landing Version System` never read as implemented proof? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 3, §FR-014, §FR-033, §SC-004]
- [ ] CHK014 Do the theme requirements stay consistent between the named theme set, persistence rules, first-visit behavior, and route-to-route behavior? [Consistency, Spec §FR-022, §FR-023, §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK015 Are public GitHub visibility rules consistent between contact requirements, public-safety constraints, and repository-exposure prohibitions? [Consistency, Spec §FR-009, §FR-010, §Scope & Trust Guardrails]
- [ ] CHK016 Are typed-content-layer and Payload-boundary requirements consistent across functional requirements and assumptions, without leaving room for premature runtime CMS dependency in MVP1? [Consistency, Spec §FR-026, §FR-027, §Assumptions]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK017 Can “within the first screen” be objectively verified from the current success criteria without a viewport baseline or measurement rule? [Measurability, Spec §SC-003]
- [ ] CHK018 Is one-page PDF success measurable across the intended browser scope, or is the requirement still subjective about what counts as “readable” and “clean”? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §FR-021, §SC-006, §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK019 Can EN/UK parity be audited objectively from the current acceptance criteria, or does the spec need a clearer comparison matrix for sections, labels, counts, and status treatment? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §FR-024, §SC-008]
- [ ] CHK020 Are public-safety success criteria defined broadly enough to cover screenshots, labels, architectural descriptions, and outbound proof links, not only private repository references? [Coverage, Gap, Spec §SC-009, §Scope & Trust Guardrails]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK021 Are requirements defined for what happens if one required contact method is temporarily unavailable, changed, or intentionally removed? [Gap, Spec §FR-009]
- [ ] CHK022 Are underfilled-proof scenarios addressed if fewer than six public-safe commercial examples are available or remain valid at a given time? [Gap, Spec §FR-016, §FR-032, §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK023 Are accessibility requirements specified for keyboard navigation, focus visibility, theme switching, in-page jump links, and outbound link behavior across all interactive UI? [Gap, Spec §FR-003, §FR-006, §FR-022, §FR-029]
- [ ] CHK024 Are fallback and recovery requirements defined beyond the commercial block for missing localized content, missing case-page assets, or parity drift discovered during content updates? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, §FR-024, §FR-031, §FR-032]

## Governance Alignment

- [ ] CHK025 Do the requirements explicitly protect the hiring-outcome-first principle by defining which homepage and case-page content is mandatory versus distracting or low-signal? [Traceability, Constitution §I, Spec §FR-003, §FR-007, §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK026 Are public-safety exclusions documented clearly enough for every proof source so future edits cannot accidentally introduce private repo links, confidential brand framing, or internal architecture details? [Traceability, Constitution §II, Spec §FR-010, §FR-018, §FR-025]
- [ ] CHK027 Does the parity requirement preserve equal hiring signal in both languages without allowing one locale to become a secondary or stale variant during MVP1 iteration? [Traceability, Constitution §III, Spec §FR-024, §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK028 Does the spec keep MVP1 small and verifiable by clearly separating active deliverables from deferred work, or are any boundaries still loose enough to invite MVP2/MVP3 drift? [Traceability, Constitution §V, Spec §Active Release Slice, §FR-030]

## Notes

- Locked MVP1 decisions should only be revisited if this checklist surfaces a real contradiction in the written requirements.
- Use this checklist to improve requirement quality, not to verify runtime behavior.
